runs.reasoningSummary: I think the relevant intensive-margin Frisch elasticity for employed prime-age U.S. workers is usually below 1 and often centered around the mid-0.3 to 0.5 range in macro/lifecycle calibration. I put some probability on very low elasticities from modern micro evidence, but allow a right tail because model specification and identification choices can generate larger values.
